violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. -Per interview of PIC, he is in the process of signing up for an online course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026
The knob to the hot water at the back hand-washing sink was observed in poor repair. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The non-food contact surfaces underneath the cook line have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The following observed in poor repair: -floor tiles in the back area were observed cracked. -the floor and wall junctures were observed not coved. -the ceiling tiles observed stained and no longer durable. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2026

The Certified Food Safety Manager License associated with the establishment is currently expired (Expired 03/24). Certified Food Safety Manager License is to be renewed. Application printed and supplied to the owner at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

Several foods stored in the Prep Unit were observed held at 46-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

The following equipment was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment... - The back hand sink faucet hot water handle was observed in need of replacement - The Prep Unit across from the flat-top grill was observed to be iced over and not maintaining proper ambient temperature for Time/Temperature Control for Safety (TCS) Foods Equipment is to be maintained in good repair and properly adjusted for use as intended. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The non-food-contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Cookline Equipment surfaces - Refrigeration surfaces (internal/external) - Various storage surfaces throughout the kitchen area Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil and residue. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2026

Several areas of the floor, walls, ceiling in the facility were observed in need of repair... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and/or missing exposing the subfloor below - Floor-to-wall junctures were observed to be missing and separating from the wall - Wall coverings were observed to be exposing inner wall structure at several corners and base of walls - The floor behind the hot water heater and mop sink area was observed to be in need of repair (water damage) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2026

The floor and walls under and behind equipment in the kitchen cookline, refrigeration, and storage areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of storage shelving and undersides of equipment were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

Several areas of the floor, walls, ceiling in the facility were observed in need of repair... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and/or missing - Floor-to-wall junctures were observed to be missing and separating from the wall - Wall coverings were observed to be exposing inner wall structure at several corners and base of walls The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2025

At the time of inspection, live and dead cockroaches were observed in the kitchen area behind equipment. Per the PIC, pest control is due to return in 2 weeks for continued treatment. PIC is to remove dead insects as observed and to continue to follow the guidance of the pest control program that is currently in place. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

Several areas of the floor, walls, ceiling in the facility were observed in need of repair... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and/or missing - Floor-to-wall junctures were observed to be missing and separating from the wall - Wall coverings were observed to be exposing inner wall structure at several corners and base of walls The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2025

The presence of insects (Cockroaches) is not controlled as evidenced by live cockroaches observed moving around the kitchen area and on traps. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. At the time of inspection, pest control was contacted and set to arrive same day as inspection. PIC is to submit pest control plan of action to RIDOH CFP Inspector and Supervisor via email. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The non-food-contact surfaces of Cookline and refrigeration equipment in the kitchen and storage area were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues and to aid in controlling pests. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Several areas of the floor, walls, ceiling in the facility were observed in need of repair... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and/or missing - Floor-to-wall junctures were observed to be missing and separating from the wall - Wall coverings were observed to be exposing inner wall structure at several corners and base of walls The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage room area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024

The floor and walls under and behind equipment in the kitchen and storage areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in pest control. Per the PIC, thorough cleaning is to begin same day. Professional Cleaning Company was contacted at time of inspection and set to arrive same day as inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2024

Several areas of the floor, walls, ceiling in the facility were observed in need of repair... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and/or missing - Floor-to-wall junctures were observed to be missing - Wall coverings were observed to be exposing inner wall structure at several corners and base of walls - The ceiling over the food prep sink area was observed to be missing a tile exposing the inn structure above The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage room area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2024

The the floor and walls under and behind equipment in the kitchen and storage areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC is to use the 3 bay sink for proper sanitizing of equipment until dish machine is adjusted/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigeration (internal/external surfaces) were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2024

Several areas of the floor, walls, ceiling in the facility were observed in need of repair... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and/or missing - Floor-to-wall junctures were observed to be missing - Wall coverings were observed to be exposing inner wall structure at several corners and base of walls - The ceiling over the food prep sink area was observed to be missing a tile exposing the inn structure above The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

The the floor and walls under and behind equipment in the kitchen and storage areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage room area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 25 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC stated they can adjust sanitizer. Establishment to use 3-bay until sanitizer is adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. PIC, stated they will add stickers to menu as a temporary solution until new menus are printed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

Multiple foods were observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Food was still frozen and moved to refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

The reach-in unit was observed holding food inside @ 42 degrees. Ambient was observed 39-43 degree. Establishment to get unit serviced/adjusted. Establishment to store TCS foods in other refrigeration units until unit is serviced/adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the interior of multiple refrigeration units have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
Old rodent droppings were observed underneath the shelf in the storage area. The premises shall be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage area next to the dry storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
